title: Transport
shortSummary: Buy tickets and use France's trains, metro, and buses with confidence.
culturalNote: France has an excellent rail network, with the high-speed TGV connecting cities quickly. In Paris the metro is dense and easy, and tickets or a Navigo pass cover the network. Remember to validate (composter) older paper tickets where required. People queue politely and a friendly Bonjour to the driver or ticket seller goes a long way.

grammar.explanation: Use prendre (to take) for transport: je prends le bus, je prends le train. To say 'by', use en for most vehicles (en train, en voiture) but à for foot or bike: à pied, à vélo.

examples.native: Je prends le métro tous les jours.
examples.en: I take the metro every day.

grammar.commonMistake: Using à for a vehicle: it is en voiture and en train, but à pied and à vélo.

grammar.explanation: devoir (must, to have to) expresses obligation and takes an infinitive: Je dois changer à Lyon (I have to change at Lyon). Forms: je dois, tu dois, il doit, nous devons, vous devez, ils doivent.

examples.en: I have to buy a ticket.

grammar.commonMistake: Conjugating the second verb. After devoir it stays an infinitive: je dois partir.

dialogue.setup: A traveller buys a train ticket at the station counter.

lines.speaker: Voyageur
lines.native: Bonjour, un billet pour Marseille, s'il vous plaît.
lines.english: Hello, a ticket to Marseille, please.

lines.speaker: Voyageur
lines.english: Return. Do I have to change?

lines.speaker: Guichet
lines.native: Oui, une fois à Lyon. Le train part à dix heures.
lines.english: Yes, once at Lyon. The train leaves at ten.

lines.speaker: Guichet
lines.native: Quai numéro quatre. Ça fait quatre-vingt-dix euros.

lines.speaker: Voyageur
lines.english: Thank you. Have a good day!

quiz.question: How do you say 'I take the metro'?
quiz.explanation: prendre is used for transport: je prends le métro.

quiz.question: Nous voyageons _____ train. (by)
quiz.explanation: Most vehicles take en: en train, en voiture.

quiz.question: Arrange: train / part / le / quand
quiz.explanation: Quand (when) + part le train (the train leaves).
